text: The Problem of Stunting In toddlers in Indonesia and Challenges and Solutions in the New Era. ABSTRACT Stunting which is a stunted growth on children under five years old is one of the severe problems in Indonesia. This problem gives very severe long term impacts on children’s health and development. In this research the author uses qualitative descriptive method. From the data analysis it is found that although the rate of stunting in Indonesia is slowly reduced, it is still very high. In addition to that there are many challenges such as: long term malnutrition, ineffective parenting skills, lack of knowledge about food balance, lack of postpartum care, recurring infectious diseases in children, and poor sanitation. In order to solve this problem, a special and sensitive intervention is needed in the form of a cross-sectoral approach, strengthening women, as well as in the form of policies and programs to support the prevention of stunting. Stunting is one of the severe problems in Indonesia that requires a coordination of multi-sectoral approach. In the solution needed in this era is a sustainable intervention strategy in order to solve the stunting in order that children’s growth and development in Indonesia will be optimal. Keywords: Toddlers, Nutrition, Prevalence, Solutions, Stunting, Challenges
explanation: The text uses generic, boilerplate phrasing with broad claims and lacks concrete data or specific methods, a pattern often seen in AI-generated abstracts. Some subtle oddities in phrasing and capitalization also hint at automated generation rather than a polished human-authored abstract.
